stars.description: Symphony turns project work into isolated, autonomous implementation runs, allowing teams to manage work instead of supervising coding agents.
stars.highlights: Symphony enables teams to manage project work through isolated, autonomous implementation runs rather than directly supervising coding agents. It provides a framework for turning project specifications into self-contained execution units that can operate independently.
stars.worthReading: This project offers a novel approach to managing AI-assisted development workflows at scale, particularly valuable for teams looking to integrate autonomous coding agents into their processes without constant supervision.
stars.scoreReason: Freshness: 2 (established OpenAI repo with fresh angle), Relevance: 3 (AI agent infrastructure), Concreteness: 1 (description is somewhat concrete but lacks implementation details)

stars.description: Practical, Colab-friendly notebooks for fine-tuning and running audio AI models
stars.highlights: Smol-audio provides practical, Colab-friendly notebooks for fine-tuning and running audio AI models, making advanced audio AI accessible without heavy infrastructure. It focuses on hands-on experimentation with state-of-the-art audio models.
stars.worthReading: It's worth exploring for its low-barrier entry to audio AI fine-tuning, ideal for practitioners wanting to quickly experiment with audio models in a familiar notebook environment.
stars.scoreReason: Freshness is moderate (240 stars, rising but not explosive), relevance is decent (audio AI fine-tuning is niche but within AI tooling), and concreteness is high (working notebooks).

interestSummary: Merve Noyan has recently shown interest in autonomous coding agent orchestration and practical audio AI model fine-tuning, reflecting a focus on developer tooling and multimodal AI applications.
